title: LiveMap
description: Output of Step 7's site-explorer pre-pass: a per-route map of what actually exists on the running SUT, captured via Playwright MCP. Consumed by Step 7 planning (render_live_map_for_prompt) and Step 8 codegen (render_live_map_for_codegen, build_observed_dev_pool) as a non-authoritative grounding/locator-seed source — codegen still emits tbd("intent") sentinels resolved at Step 9 runtime.

role.description: Accessible role, as read from the AOM (e.g. button, link, textbox). May be 'generic' or similar for a roleless clickable captured via the text/CSS fallback.

name.description: Accessible name, captured verbatim from the AOM. For a roleless clickable this is the visible text used to anchor the locator.

locator.description: Verified locator object: the HIGHEST-priority candidate (per the CLAUDE.md ladder id>test_id>role>label>placeholder>text>alt>title>css) that the DOM probe confirmed resolves to EXACTLY ONE element on the page. Null (or omitted) only when NO candidate is verified-unique — in which case locator_ambiguous must be set. This is the authoritative locator field going forward; test_id is retained for backward compatibility with the dev-pool path.

strategy.description:
Locator strategy, following the CLAUDE.md priority ladder in that exact order. Maps to a Playwright getter: id/test_id -> get_by_test_id or #id; role -> get_by_role(role, name=); label/placeholder/text/alt/title -> the matching get_by_* getter; css -> a scoped CSS selector (NEVER XPath). 'text' also serves the roleless-clickable fallback (e.g. a launcher tile that is a

inside a clickable

with no ARIA role) — verified unique among the page's clickables.

value.description: The locator value: the attribute value for id/test_id, the accessible name for role, the label/placeholder/text/alt/title string for those, or the CSS selector for css.

name.description: Accessible name, present for strategy=role so the runtime can call get_by_role(role, name=...). Usually equal to the element's name field.

verified_unique.description: True when the DOM probe confirmed this candidate matches exactly one element on the page (the 'one result in the devtools search box' check). A locator object is only emitted when this is true.

test_id.description: DOM attribute value (id/data-testid/data-test/data-cy/data-qa/name), populated ONLY when a browser_evaluate probe confirmed it uniquely identifies this element on the page. Never a value inferred/guessed from the accessible name or context. Null when no such attribute was found or verified unique. Retained for backward compatibility with the JIT dev-pool path; the authoritative field going forward is `locator`.

locator_ambiguous.description: Present and true only when NEITHER a unique DOM attribute NOR a unique role+name pair could be established for this element — a genuine SUT testability/accessibility gap, not a fabricated locator. Absent (not false) when not applicable.

ambiguity_reason.description: Short human-readable explanation of why the element is ambiguous (e.g. a data-test value shared across sibling elements, or an icon-only control with no accessible name). Only meaningful when locator_ambiguous is true.

base_url.description: SUT origin the crawl was run against.

_telemetry.description: Per-run counters emitted by the deterministic driver (steps/s07/live_driver.py). Absent when the map came from the site-explorer LLM agent path. Diagnostic only — never used for planning decisions.

routes_truncated_by_cap.description: Number of plan entries dropped because the plan exceeded QTEA_LIVE_EXPLORE_MAX_ROUTES. Nonzero = silent coverage loss; Step 7 architect logs a warning when this is > 0.

reveal_callouts.description: Number of LLM callouts to the live-explore-reveal-judge (progressive-disclosure decisions).

ambiguity_callouts.description: Number of LLM callouts to the live-explore-ambiguity-judge (locator disambiguation).

url.description: Final resolved URL, when it differs from path.

exists.description: True only if the page rendered expected app content. A route gated behind login is still exists:false + auth_required:true (it is NOT reported as truly missing).

auth_required.description: True if the route bounced to a login/SSO/identity-provider screen (no/stale session). The route almost certainly exists behind the gate.

redirected_to.description: Final URL when it differs from the requested path, else null.

discovered_from.description: The path this route was reached from. Null for seeds/root.

entry_element.description: The element ON THE PARENT (discovered_from) route that the explorer clicked to reach THIS route. Same shape as an elements[] entry. Absent/null on root routes (discovered_from == null) or when the transition wasn't a click (direct URL nav, redirect). Guarantees the reach-path navigation is captured with a verified locator so codegen's beforeEach / setup has an authoritative locator for the launcher tile / nav item that led here — even when it is a roleless clickable that AOM alone would miss. Recorded in ADDITION to (not instead of) inclusion in the parent route's elements[]; the entry_element form makes the parent->child edge explicit.

snapshot_source.description: How this route's elements were captured. 'aom' (default, omitted) = the accessibility tree, always preferred. 'iframe_full' = a frame-scoped/full snapshot taken because a tested element lives inside an <iframe> the AOM does not reach (Hard Rule exception 1). 'raw_dom_fallback' = the full-DOM last-resort tier, used ONLY when AOM + the scoped attribute probe could not yield a verified-unique locator for a needed element (Hard Rule exception 2). Both non-aom values are gated by the $10 cost ceiling and must carry a fallback_reason.

fallback_reason.description: Short human-readable reason the route needed a non-AOM snapshot_source (e.g. 'target Save button lives inside a payment <iframe>' or 'no unique attribute or role+name for the grid row-action menu; full DOM read to recover a scoped CSS locator').

elements.description: Comprehensive list of interactive/salient elements captured on this route, including elements revealed inside a non-destructively opened dialog/form.
